concept: Calibrate / Compare and Relate an Instrument to a Reference
description: To relate an instrument to a stated reference, and to check or adjust that relation. Calibrating is narrower than a full inspection, and a device can be calibrated without the whole system around it ever being examined.
sound_symbolism: The measured opening 'masu' recalls laying a device's indication alongside a stated reference. Final 'ro' resolves that comparison into an enduring relation that can be checked again. The movement from comparison to stable relation suits calibration without implying perfect accuracy.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia ha keli masuro.' (I calibrate this device.) Name the reference, conditions, date, and exact result separately where required. The event-noun rule supplies calibration. Distinct from 'masue' (measure), 'theali' (fitting), 'telua' (accurate), and 'nilaki' (inspect).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Calibration treats measurement as a maintained relation with conditions and uncertainty rather than an unconditioned revelation of fact.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Reliable craft has long depended on checking tools against known references and recording when that relation changes.

tags.activity: comparison and adjustment of a device against a reference
tags.cognition: a practice that establishes how an indication relates to a reference
tags.creation: maintenance of measuring and signaling tools
